lessonTitle: Guiding Principles — What is Volunteer Engagement
lessonDescription: Volunteer engagement, also known as volunteer management, refers to the systematic and logical process of working with and through volunteers to achieve an organization's objectives.

slides.title: Guiding Principles: What is Volunteer Engagement?

data.html:
What is Volunteer Engagement?

Definition: Volunteer engagement, also known as volunteer management, refers to the systematic and logical process of working with and through volunteers to achieve an organization's objectives. Volunteer engagement can be grouped into four stages: Recruitment, Orientation & Training, Recognition, and Stewardship.

Volunteer engagement typically involves:

items.html: Leading and supervising volunteers in defined roles
Roles can include: community ambassadors, program support, information holders, liaisons, etc.

data.html:
Role of the Volunteer Manager

The volunteer manager's core responsibilities:

items.html: Know your volunteers — Get to know them so that you can provide them with a rewarding experience
Match skills to needs — Ensure the skills, interests, and experiences of volunteers are appropriately matched to the needs of the organization
Support and recognize — Ensure volunteers are well-trained, matched to meaningful roles, and appropriately recognized

data.question: What else is volunteer engagement referred to as?
data.feedback_correct: Correct! Volunteer engagement is also known as volunteer management — the systematic process of working with volunteers to achieve organizational objectives.
data.feedback_incorrect: Not quite. Review the definition of volunteer engagement from the previous slide.
